title: IBM’s 25% crash reveals AI’s hidden corporate casualty
excerpt: IBM’s board faced an uncomfortable decision after learning that the company’s second quarter had fallen well short of expectations. Directors can now take a...
content_html:
IBM's board faced an uncomfortable decision after learning that the company's second quarter had fallen well short of expectations.

Directors can now take a breather and wait until the scheduled earnings release and let executives explain the results in detail. Or they could warn investors immediately and risk a violent market reaction.

They opted for disclosure.

IBM (IBM) shares fell more than 25% on July 14, wiping off approximately $69 billion in market value in the company's worst one-day drop ever. IBM's market valuation is around $202.5 billion, after the stock ended July 17 at $212.67.

The early data were disappointing but not bad enough on their own to justify the magnitude of the selloff.

IBM estimates revenue for the second quarter to be $17.2 billion, up 1% from a year earlier, and operating earnings of $2.93 a share. Wall Street was looking for about $17.86 billion in sales and $3.02 per share, according to LSEG statistics as cited by Reuters.

What spooked investors was the cause for the shortfall.

Customers shifted spending to servers, storage, and memory that are needed for artificial intelligence infrastructure. And little hardware went to the front of corporate purchase queues, slowing large IBM software and mainframe-related deals.

That suggests a risk that extends well beyond one particularly poor quarter.

Artificial intelligence can weaken IBM without replacing it. It can affect IBM by reducing the technological budgets its clients once spent on traditional software, consulting, and mainframe systems.

"This quarter we faltered," IBM CEO Arvind Krishna wrote in his letter to investors.

IBM's warning broke its turnaround narrative

IBM had a lot of momentum going into the quarter.

First-quarter revenue rose 9% to $15.9 billion. Software revenue increased 11%, infrastructure advanced 15%, and IBM Z revenue jumped 51%. Management maintained its expectation for more than 5% constant-currency revenue growth in 2026 and approximately $1 billion of additional annual free cash flow.

But the preliminary second-quarter data altered that tale quickly.

Software grew at a slower rate of 5%. Consulting revenue was flat. Infrastructure revenue decreased 7%, below management's prior guidance for a low single-digit decline as the first IBM z17 mainframe launch cycle developed.

IBM claimed the infrastructure drop was worse than expected due to inadequate Z system performance and related transaction processing software.

That counts.

IBM did not say large acquisitions had failed or businesses newly acquired were crumbling. Krishna said HashiCorp and Confluent performed well, while Red Hat revenue growth accelerated to 11%.

sentences.zh: 咨询收入持平。

sentences.en: Krishna said HashiCorp and Confluent performed well, while Red Hat revenue growth accelerated to 11%.
sentences.zh: Krishna 表示，HashiCorp 和 Confluence 表现良好，而红帽收入增长加速至 11%。

content_html_zh:
在得知公司第二季度业绩远低于预期后，IBM 董事会面临着一个令人不安的决定。

董事们现在可以喘口气，等待预定的收益发布，让高管详细解释结果。 或者他们可以立即警告投资者并冒着市场剧烈反应的风险。

他们选择了披露。

IBM (IBM) 股价 7 月 14 日下跌超过 25%，市值蒸发约 690 亿美元，创下该公司有史以来最严重的单日跌幅。 IBM 股票 7 月 17 日收盘价为 212.67 美元，其市场估值约为 2025 亿美元。

早期数据令人失望，但其本身还不足以证明抛售规模的合理性。

IBM预计第二季度营收为172亿美元，同比增长1%，每股运营收益为2.93美元。 据路透社援引伦敦证券交易所集团的统计数据，华尔街预计销售额约为 178.6 亿美元，每股收益为 3.02 美元。

令投资者感到恐慌的是造成资金短缺的原因。

客户将支出转向人工智能基础设施所需的服务器、存储和内存。 企业采购队列中排在前面的硬件很少，从而减缓了大型 IBM 软件和大型机相关交易的速度。

这表明风险远远超出了特别糟糕的季度。

人工智能可以削弱 IBM，但不会取代它。 它可以通过减少客户曾经花在传统软件、咨询和大型机系统上的技术预算来影响 IBM。

IBM 首席执行官 Arvind Krishna 在给投资者的信中写道：“本季度我们表现不佳。”

IBM 的警告打破了其扭亏为盈的说法

IBM 在本季度表现强劲。

第一季度收入增长 9%，达到 159 亿美元。 软件收入增长 11%，基础设施增长 15%，IBM Z 收入增长 51%。 管理层维持 2026 年固定汇率收入增长超过 5% 以及年度自由现金流约 10 亿美元的预期。

但第二季度的初步数据很快改变了这个故事。

软件增长速度较慢，仅为 5%。 咨询收入持平。 随着第一个 IBM z17 大型机发布周期的发展，基础设施收入下降了 7%，低于管理层先前设定的低个位数下降的指导。

IBM 声称，由于 Z 系统性能和相关事务处理软件不足，基础设施下降情况比预期更严重。

这很重要。

IBM 并未表示大型收购已失败或新收购的业务正在崩溃。 Krishna 表示，HashiCorp 和 Confluence 表现良好，而红帽收入增长加速至 11%。
